raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Citibank regarding a disputed transaction with Home Depot in the amount of {$1600.00} for six ( 6 ) custom window blinds received on XX/XX/year>. 
Issue Summary : The merchandise received is non-conforming and fails to meet the contractual specifications. Pursuant to the Uniform Commercial Code ( UCC ), I have formally rejected the delivery due to the following material defects : Incorrect Specifications : Control arms were placed in the center ; the order explicitly required left-side placement. 
Missing Components : The order arrived with two fewer blades than specified per unit.

Physical Damage : One head rail arrived damaged and is entirely unusable. 
Chronology of Failed Resolution : XX/XX/year> : Upon delivery, I contacted customer service. Representative " XXXX XXXX '' acknowledged the issues and issued a {$150.00} credit for the inconvenience. 
Subsequent Action : Citibank/Home Depot later reversed this {$150.00} credit without my authorization or a valid explanation. 
XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, XXXX  : I contacted " XXXX '' multiple times to request a full refund and schedule a freight pickup for the rejected goods. These requests have been ignored. 
The Dispute : Citibank has failed to provide a permanent credit despite the merchants failure to deliver the contracted goods or provide a means for return ( freight pickup ). The merchant is essentially holding my funds hostage for damaged, incorrect, and incomplete merchandise while refusing to retrieve the items.
